title: MPA Driver Profile (v2)
description: First production-targeted driver-profile schema. v2 supersedes the v0.x bootstrap series (v0.x preserved as reference but is not authoritative for production consumers). The seven-section structure remains from RFC-S v0.2 §4; v2's new content is a pinned `translation_field` shape, derived from mpa-auditor §Q13 (forward-only architecture) and the three reference-substrate-class instances in mpa-conform's seed corpus. Substrate-specific values (envelope axes, intent operations, reference_outputs payloads) remain open-typed; only the cross-substrate exchange contract is pinned.

regime_classifier_axis.description: Which axis the substrate walks to traverse c→s→r. 'drive': regime classified by drive level relative to threshold (e.g. LEDs by V vs V_th, lasers by pump vs threshold). 'damping': regime classified by Q at structural resonance (e.g. RLC, voice coil actuators, viscoelastic). Sets the substrate-conditional interpretation of cdv1 §3 limit-point status: drive-axis carries irreducible thermodynamic s-window floor (nkT/q for diodes; Schawlow–Townes broadening for lasers); damping-axis carries measurement-limited width that vanishes in the deterministic limit (F-003-rlc Q=0.5 zero).

operating_envelope.description: Substrate-declared envelope: initial_conditions, parameter_ranges, measurement_protocol — what calibration must verify. Field-level structure is open per RFC-S §4 because the envelope axes vary per substrate class. The seed corpus instances axes as a named map (e.g. {T: {values:[...], units:'Tc-relative'}}) plus n_cells and xdot_choices arrays; v2 does not pin this shape, but consumers can expect 'axes' (object) and 'xdot_choices' (array of strings) on every v2 profile.

gamut.description: RG-trajectory image in canonical-representation space. May include s_window_width_formula (string, substrate-physics formula for δ) and s_window_width_floor (number, V or substrate-natural units; numerical value at characterization conditions). For drive-axis substrates these characterize the irreducible thermodynamic floor on the s-window; for damping-axis substrates they characterize the measurement-noise-limited floor (0 in the deterministic limit).

translation_field.description: Forward map only (canonical → substrate-native). The backward map is structurally ill-posed (rank-deficiency, conditioning failure, observable insufficiency all live on the backward side) and never built. See mpa-auditor §Q13 forward-only architecture; RFC-S §4 'translation_field' section; RFC-S Appendix B item 1 (tangent-flow / continuous-form deferred). v2 ships the discrete lookup_table shape only.

direction.description: v2 is forward-only by architecture, not as a parameter. Any future 'backward' value requires a v3 schema with a paired ill-posedness-reporting contract.

shape.description: v2 ships lookup_table. tangent-flow / continuous-form are RFC-S Appendix B item 1, deferred until a substrate's drift actually forces them.

description.description: Free-text. The seed-corpus convention is to cite the Q13 reference and the leading-order method here.

rule.description: One entry per (operating_point × xdot_choice) cell. Cardinality is operating_envelope.n_cells × |operating_envelope.xdot_choices|.

reference_outputs.description: Per-cell reference outputs the curator path commits. Item shape is open at v2 (the seed corpus uses {data_upload_id, data_upload_path, operating_point, xdot_choice, tier}); v2 pins only that the array is non-empty so round-trip validation per RFC-S §5 has at least one anchor.

xdot_choice.description: Substrate-native ẋ-choice this rule entry maps from. Must appear in operating_envelope.xdot_choices.

operating_point.description: Identifies a cell in the substrate's operating envelope. `label` and `gt` are cross-substrate; the substrate-specific axis values (T, p_base, h_field, scenario, etc.) ride additionalProperties — the union shape used by the seed corpus (with nulls for irrelevant axes) is one valid convention; a substrate may also omit irrelevant keys entirely.

label.description: Human-readable operating-point identifier. Must match the corresponding reference_outputs entries.

gt.description: Ground-truth regime at this operating point. cdv1 §3: c = committed/cooperative, s = sustained/critical, r = relaxed/below-threshold, k = k_frust closed-chain frustration.

canonical_point.description: Canonical-coordinate target the forward map projects to. `chit` is the load-bearing required field; the other three are co-required by the seed-corpus convention because they cover the cdv1 universal-two-mode-kernel parameters that the auditor's inversion engine reads. Additional canonical fields (further cdv1 API-slot values per mpa-auditor §Q6) ride additionalProperties.

chit.description: Canonical drive coordinate. cdv1 §3 chit unit. Sign convention is substrate-class-conditional and declared by the leading-order method (e.g. chit = Tc - T for ck-glassy; chit = ln(p_threshold/p_base) for surface-code-qec).

gamma_AB.description: Cross-coupling. cdv1 §Universal two-mode kernel. Sign: < 0 cooperative, > 0 competitive, 0 = decoupled.

k_frust.description: Whether this operating point carries a k_frust closed-chain frustration subgraph. cdv1 §3 / RFC-S §1.

method.description: Provenance of this canonical assignment. The seed corpus convention is a short prose form: 'leading_order: <formula>' for curator-path assignments, with a free form reserved for measurement-anchored values once the researcher path lands.
